Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Court Order And Motion For Reconsideration And Rehearing Of Special Motion To Strike Based On True Facts, Request To File Opposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Karen Matcke Crosby
Off Calendar Nov-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF KAREN CROSBY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Court Order And Motion For Reconsideration And Rehearing Of Special Motion To Strike Based On True Facts, Request To File Opposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Karen Matcke Crosby.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Karen M. Crosby, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
Tenny Mirzaryan, Esq., of KELLER FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP, (818) 342-7442, for Specially Appearing Defendant Bethania Baciagalupe.
David Nemecek, Esq., of THE FORTRESS LAW FIRM, INC., (415) 277-5400, for Defendants TENNY MIRZAYAN, STEPHEN M. FISHBACK AND KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP.

Ruling is argued. At conclusion of argument, and having reviewed the pleadings, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for reconsideration is denied, and Bacigalupe's request for fees is granted in part. The motion for reconsideration is denied for all of these reasons, each of which alone suffices to deny the relief: (1) The order granting the motion to strike was the equivalent of a judgment and this court is divested of jurisdiction. Marshall v. Webster (2020) 54 Cal.App.5th 275, 283 ("an order granting a special motion to strike is an appealable order and is expressly deemed a judgment for purposes of the rule governing appeal deadlines"). See also, e.g., Melbostad v. Fisher (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 987, 994. [The court is not, however, divested of jurisdiction to award attorneys fees. E.g., Carpenter v. Jack in the Box Corp. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 454, 461.] (2) The motion for reconsideration was filed October 12, 2023 (although it was signed October 3 and 4-both dates appear in different places), which is more than 10 days after the filing and service of the order the subject of the motion. CCP 1008. (3) The court has reviewed the motion and its accompanying papers, and finds no reason to reconsider on its own motion. Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094. (4) Ms. Crosby had a full opportunity to prepare and file an opposition to the motion to strike. Just as parties successful on a special motion to strike are entitled to attorney's fees, so too are they entitled on motions such as this for reconsideration of those rulings. I note that the central reasons for denying this motion listed above, which are previewed in the Opposition, are uncontested in the reply papers, which underscores the motion's lack of merit. Fees in the amount of $12475 are sought, and supported by the declaration of D. Dukelow dated October 20, 2023 at pages 21-22. While the fees sought now are high for a routine motion for reconsideration, my review of Ms. Crosby's papers shows they are difficult to parse, contain ad hominem attacks, make conflicting allegations, and spend inordinate space on irrelevant issues. I agree with Mr. Dukelow that an inordinate amount of time was reasonably required to respond to these papers. Incivility including personal attacks on the opposing side drives up the cost of litigation and is properly considered in determining the reasonableness of fees. Snoeck v. Exaktime Innovations, Inc. (Oct. 2, 2023, No. B321566) 2023 WL 7014096, at *11. The court awards $10,000 in fees in favor of defendants to be paid by Karen Crosby. This must be paid not later than December 8, 2023.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.13.12.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR: 13859, T: 714.471.2597. (302/CEK)
